## Freelance Service Agreement

### Preamble

This Freelance Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into on 2025-01-15 ("Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., a corporation having a principal place of business in California, represented by Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, represented by Alex Chen, Senior Designer ("Freelancer").

### Recitals

WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Freelancer to perform design services in connection with Client's project titled "Brand Refresh 2025"; and WHEREAS, Freelancer has the skills, experience, and ability to perform such services and desires to provide the services to Client under the terms set forth herein; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

---

## Clauses (21 total)

### Scope of Services

*Type: standard*

Freelancer shall provide a complete visual identity redesign for Client's "Brand Refresh 2025," including logo concepts, logo refinement, a brand guidelines document, and an asset package (collectively, the "Services"). The Services shall conform to the descriptions, deliverables, and due dates set forth in Clause 3 (Deliverables and Schedule). Any work beyond the Scope of Services shall require a written change order agreed by both parties and may incur additional fees.

### Term

*Type: standard*

This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until completion of the Services or termination in accordance with Clause 11. The Services shall be completed by 2025-03-15 ("Deadline"), subject to adjustments by mutual written agreement or delay caused by Client or Force Majeure.

### Deliverables and Schedule

*Type: standard*

Freelancer will deliver the following deliverables according to the schedule and amounts shown: 
- Logo concepts (3 options) — due Jan 30, 2025 — $2,000
- Selected logo refinement — due Feb 15, 2025 — $1,500
- Brand guidelines document — due Mar 1, 2025 — $2,500
- Asset package delivery — due Mar 15, 2025 — $2,500
Each deliverable shall include the file formats and resolution reasonably required for Client's intended use as discussed between the parties. Timely Client feedback is required to meet scheduled dates.

### Fees and Payment Terms

*Type: standard*

Total Fee: $8,500. Client shall pay a non‑refundable deposit of $2,550 (30% of Total Fee) upon execution of this Agreement. Remaining payments shall be made on a milestone basis as follows: payment for Logo concepts — $2,000 due upon delivery of that milestone; payment for Selected logo refinement — $1,500 due upon delivery of that milestone; payment for Brand guidelines document — $2,500 due upon delivery of that milestone; payment for Asset package delivery — $2,500 due upon delivery of that milestone. Freelancer will invoice Client for each milestone upon delivery; invoices are payable within 15 days of receipt unless otherwise agreed in writing. Late payments shall accrue interest at the lesser of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law. All amounts are payable in U.S. dollars.

### Revisions

*Type: standard*

The fee includes up to three (3) rounds of reasonable revision for each deliverable. Additional revisions requested by Client beyond the included three rounds will be billed at Freelancer's standard hourly rate (if not pre-stated, to be agreed in writing prior to performing such additional work). A revision round is a single cycle of Client feedback followed by Freelancer's updated submission.

### Client Responsibilities

*Type: standard*

Client shall provide timely feedback, approvals, source materials, brand content, and any other information reasonably necessary for Freelancer to perform the Services. If Client fails to respond within five (5) business days to requests for approvals or information, Freelancer may treat the last set of written instructions as approved and may invoice for work performed. Delays caused by Client may result in schedule adjustments and additional fees.

### Ownership, Copyright, and License

*Type: standard*

Subject to Client's full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, Freelancer assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the final delivered deliverables ("Final Deliverables") created specifically for Client under this Agreement, excluding Freelancer's preexisting materials, general concepts, ideas, templates, tools, software, or methodologies (collectively, "Freelancer Materials"). Freelancer retains all right, title, and interest in Freelancer Materials. Freelancer grants Client a perpetual, worldwide, royalty‑free license to use any Freelancer Materials included in the Final Deliverables to the extent necessary for Client's use of the Final Deliverables. Freelancer may display the Final Deliverables and work‑in‑progress in Freelancer's portfolio and for self-promotion unless Client provides written notice that certain materials are confidential trade secrets (subject to Clause 10).

### Warranties and Representations

*Type: standard*

Each party represents and warrants that it has the full power and authority to enter into this Agreement. Freelancer warrants that the Final Deliverables delivered to Client are original to Freelancer or properly licensed for use by Client and that to Freelancer's knowledge, use of the Final Deliverables by Client in accordance with this Agreement will not infringe upon the intellectual property rights of any third party. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH HEREIN, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

### Indemnification

*Type: standard*

Client shall indemnify, defend and hold Freelancer harmless from and against any losses, liabilities, damages and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of Client's use of the Final Deliverables in a manner not authorized by this Agreement or based on Client-supplied materials. Freelancer shall indemnify, defend and hold Client harmless from and against any losses, liabilities, damages and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from Freelancer's breach of Clause 8 (Warranties) or from Freelancer's willful infringement of a third party's intellectual property rights in the delivered work. The indemnifying party's obligations are conditioned upon the indemnified party providing prompt written notice of a claim, reasonable cooperation, and the ability to control the defense and settlement of the claim.

### Confidentiality

*Type: standard*

Each party shall keep confidential and not disclose to any third party any nonpublic proprietary or confidential information of the other party disclosed in connection with this Agreement ("Confidential Information"), except as required by law or with the disclosing party's prior written consent. Confidential Information does not include information that is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party or information independently developed by the receiving party without reference to the disclosing party's Confidential Information. Upon request, Confidential Information (other than deliverables already paid for and delivered) will be returned or destroyed.

### Termination

*Type: standard*

Either party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other party if the breaching party fails to cure the breach within 14 days of written notice. Client may terminate for convenience upon 7 days' written notice; in such event, Freelancer shall be paid for all Services performed and expenses incurred through the effective date of termination, including a reasonable portion of partially completed work. If Client fails to pay amounts when due, Freelancer may suspend Services until payment is made. Termination does not relieve Client of the obligation to pay for Services rendered prior to termination.

### Limitation of Liability

*Type: standard*

IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, SPECIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. THE AGGREGATE LIABILITY OF EITHER PARTY FOR ANY CLAIM ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNTS PAID BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

### Independent Contractor

*Type: standard*

Freelancer is an independent contractor and nothing in this Agreement creates an employer-employee, partnership, joint venture, or agency relationship. Freelancer is responsible for payment of all federal, state, and local taxes and any other statutory obligations arising from payments made to Freelancer.

### Taxes

*Type: standard*

Client will not withhold any taxes from payments to Freelancer. Freelancer shall be solely responsible for all taxes, assessments, and other charges arising from payments made under this Agreement.

### Force Majeure

*Type: standard*

Neither party shall be liable for delays or failures in performance resulting from causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, government action, strikes, pandemics, civil unrest, or failure of suppliers, provided the affected party gives prompt written notice and uses commercially reasonable efforts to resume performance.

### Dispute Resolution; Governing Law

*Type: standard*

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California without regard to its conflicts of law principles. The parties shall first attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through negotiation. If negotiation fails, the parties agree to submit the dispute to mediation in California before initiating arbitration or litigation. If mediation does not resolve the dispute, either party may seek relief in a court of competent jurisdiction in California. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may seek injunctive or other equitable relief to protect its intellectual property rights.

### Amendments and Waiver

*Type: standard*

Any amendment or modification to this Agreement must be in writing and signed by both parties. No waiver of any breach shall be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach.

### Severability

*Type: standard*

If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect and the parties shall negotiate in good faith to replace the invalid provision with a valid provision that achieves, to the extent possible, the original intent.

### Notices

*Type: standard*

All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and delivered by hand, nationally recognized overnight courier, certified mail (return receipt requested), or email (with confirmation of receipt) to the addresses listed below or to such other address as a party may designate by written notice. Notice is effective upon receipt.

### Entire Agreement

*Type: standard*

This Agreement, including any exhibits or attachments and any written change orders signed by both parties, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, agreements, or representations, whether written or oral.

### Counterparts and Electronic Signatures

*Type: standard*

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by electronic means (e.g., PDF, fax, or electronic signature service) shall be deemed original signatures.
